Q9 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the polar plot of two systems as shown below,

Then, which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)
As A lies to the right of (–1, 0), the Nyquist pole doesn't encircle (–1, 0) point, hence system A is stable and B cuts the real axial at (–1, 0), therefore it is marginally stable.

Q10 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The open loop pole zero plot of a negative feedback system is shown below:

The appropriate root locus plot of this system is given by

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Root locus always starts from open loop poles and terminates at open loop zeros. Also, a point on the real axis lies on the root locus if the total number of poles and zeros to the right of this point is odd. Thus, only option (a) satisfies.
